problem.summary: Normalize each row of a row-major activation matrix. RMSNorm computes x * rsqrt(mean(x^2) + epsilon) * gamma. LayerNorm computes (x - mean(x)) * rsqrt(var(x) + epsilon) * gamma + beta. The baseline assigns one thread to each row. The optimized seed assigns one block to each row and uses shared-memory reductions.
inputs: int mode where 0 is rmsnorm and 1 is layernorm
outputs: Each output row is normalized independently with per-column gamma and optional beta
constraints: Gamma and beta are contiguous vectors of length cols.
RMSNorm ignores beta in this scaffold.
The optimized seed assumes one block can cover each row with strided per-thread work.
This is a correctness-first seed; future entries should add vectorized loads, warp reductions, persistent shape specialization, and fused residual/add paths.

verification.method: Compare HIP output against a double-precision CPU reference for RMSNorm or LayerNorm. Ported from the CUDA corpus; rerun on ROCm hardware before marking passed.

curation.notes: Ported from the CUDA corpus on 2026-05-20. CUDA-origin timing artifacts were removed; rerun on ROCm hardware before making performance claims.
